eml_analysis.name: heston_stochastic_volatility
eml_analysis.insight: Heston model: vol is sqrt(v_t). sqrt(x) = exp(0.5*ln(x)): ln is depth 2, scale is depth 2, exp bumps to depth 3. Each EM simulation step is EML-3 (dominated by sqrt(v)). The semi-closed form pricing formula uses complex exponentials at depth 3. All stochastic vol models with sqrt-diffusion are EML-3.

eml_analysis.name: sabr_model
eml_analysis.insight: SABR F^0.5 = exp(0.5*ln(F)): fractional power via exp(beta*ln). ln(F) is depth 2; beta*ln(F) is depth 2; exp is depth 3. For integer beta: F^2 = F*F is depth 2 (multiply). For non-integer beta: fractional power requires ln+exp = depth 3. This is why SABR is one EML depth deeper than Heston ATM.

eml_analysis.name: merton_jump_diffusion
eml_analysis.insight: Merton jumps: J = exp(gamma + delta*Z), Z~N(0,1). exp(linear) is EML-3 (depth: linear arg → scale → exp). Pricing formula: sum of BS prices (each EML-3) × Poisson weights (EML-1). Weighted sum is EML-3 linear combination. Jump-diffusion pricing = EML-3.

eml_analysis.name: bachelier_model
eml_analysis.insight: Bachelier call: (F-K)*N(d) + sigma*sqrt(T)*n(d). sqrt(T) is depth 3; N(d) uses erf (depth 3); n(d) = exp(-d²/2) is depth 3. Bachelier and Black-Scholes both EML-3 — erf is always depth 3 regardless of the underlying process.

barrier_option.reason: Payoff uses indicator 1(min>B): non-analytic (step function) → EML-inf. But the PRICE (expectation under Q) has closed form involving N(d) terms and exponentials → EML-3.

asian_option.payoff: max(A_T - K, 0) where A_T = (1/T)*integral_0^T S_t dt
asian_option.reason: Arithmetic average A_T: under log-normal, A_T is log-normally approx. Kemna-Vorst approximation: BS with adjusted sigma → EML-3. Geometric mean Asian: exact BS → EML-3.

lookback_option.reason: Running minimum is non-analytic (piecewise path-dependent). Price formula involves N(d) terms → EML-3.

digital_option.reason: Payoff is step function → EML-inf. Price = N(d2) = (1+erf(d2/sqrt(2)))/2 → EML-2 (just N, no multiplication). Actually erf is depth 3, so digital price is EML-3.

exotic_options.key_insight: Exotic payoffs are often EML-inf (max, min, indicator are non-analytic). Exotic PRICES are EML-3 (smooth expectations integrate out the kinks). The EML depth of a derivative = depth of its PRICING FORMULA, not payoff. Risk-neutral pricing 'smooths' EML-inf payoffs to EML-3 prices.

var_normal.reason: Phi^{-1}(alpha) = sqrt(2)*erfinv(2*alpha-1) — erfinv is EML-3 via series.

cvar_normal.reason: phi(x) = exp(-x²/2)/sqrt(2*pi) is EML-3; division adds depth 4 — but phi(Phi^{-1}(alpha)) is usually precomputed.

max_drawdown.reason: Running maximum is non-analytic (same as lookback) → EML-inf.

black_scholes.key_term: N(d1), N(d2) — normal CDF via erf

bachelier.verdict: EML-3: arithmetic BM, same depth as BS

heston.verdict: EML-3 per step; semi-closed form also EML-3

sabr.verdict: EML-3: fractional power deeper than integer power

merton_jump.verdict: EML-3: jump term + BS sum, all EML-3

var_cvar_normal.verdict: EML-3: quantile of normal = inverse erf

key_insight: ALL continuous option pricing formulas are EML-3. The erf/erfinv tower (normal CDF and its inverse) is always present and always contributes depth 3. Jump models, stochastic vol, exotic options: all EML-3 in their pricing formula. EML-inf appears only in non-analytic risk measures (max drawdown, VaR of heavy tails). Finance converges at EML-3 — not because formulas are simple, but because smooth probability distributions always contribute via erf (depth 3).

findings.name: Heston/SABR/Merton all EML-3
findings.content: sqrt(v), F^beta, exp(jump) — all depth 3. All continuous pricing converges at EML-3.

findings.name: Exotic payoffs EML-inf, exotic PRICES EML-3
findings.content: max/min/indicator are non-analytic → EML-inf. Pricing integrates them out → EML-3.

findings.name: EML-3 lower bound for Gaussian pricing
findings.content: Normal CDF always contributes erf (depth 3). Cannot price with Gaussian risk-neutral at shallower depth.

findings.name: SABR smile from fractional power
findings.content: F^beta for non-integer beta is depth 3 (vs depth 2 for integer). The skew in SABR originates from this EML depth difference.

next_session.title: Abstract/Categorical — EML Monoid Structure
priorities: EML trees form a monoid under composition
EML-k classes form a filtration (EML-1 ⊆ EML-2 ⊆ ...)
Universal property: EML is the free real-analytic algebra on 1 generator
Connection to lambda calculus and type theory
EML as a programming language: what is the type of eml(x,y)?
